steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage presents a structural characteristic of the series and then contrasts two opposing interpretations of it. The question asks which answer best describes the overall structure of the passage.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — introduces a claim and provides evidence to support it: the passage presents competing views rather than building toward a single supported claim. ✗
  • Choice B — describes a problem and proposes a solution: no solution is offered; the passage lays out a debate without resolving it. ✗
  • Choice C — identifies a characteristic and presents two differing viewpoints on it: the passage names a structural feature of the series, then gives two contrasting interpretations — exactly this pattern. ✓
  • Choice D — compares two works and argues one is superior: no judgment of superiority is made; the passage remains neutral between the two viewpoints. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
